name: Voice evidence floor: fewer than 3 usable voice reference excerpts is an automatic voice_qa fail

setup.injected_fault: client has a private LinkedIn, no blog, and sparse resume prose -- researcher legitimately returns only 2 voice_reference_excerpts, honestly, not padded

steps: researcher runs linkup_client_research and returns client_research with usable_claim_count > 0 but voice_reference_excerpts.length = 2 and usable_voice_excerpt_count = 2
brand_strategist writes a thin-but-honest positioning_brief and ClientBrandMemory version 1 anyway (positioning does not depend on voice excerpt count)
copywriter and builder produce site_copy and site_draft normally, drafted only against the sourced claim_status:'supported' findings that do exist

voice_qa checks client_research.usable_voice_excerpt_count BEFORE attempting the three named checks, finds 2 < 3, and short-circuits to gate_result = 'fail' without running structural_voice_match, lexical_fingerprint_match, or claim_sourcing
assertions: voice_qa_report.findings includes at least one entry with severity 'blocker' and area referencing the voice-evidence floor (e.g. 'voice_evidence_floor'), citing usable_voice_excerpt_count = 2 explicitly, not a generic failure message
voice_qa does NOT attempt structural_voice_match, lexical_fingerprint_match, or claim_sourcing for this candidate -- the short-circuit happens before the three named checks run
no deployed_site artifact exists for client_thin_voice_001 at this point

researcher's repair attempt does not fabricate a third excerpt to hit the count -- padding voice_reference_excerpts to force a pass is itself an ANTI-FABRICATION violation, not a valid fix; a repair that still returns 2 honest excerpts and gets routed to human review is a correct outcome

name: Claim sourcing zero-tolerance: a stated claim with no resolvable source_ref auto-fails claim_sourcing

setup.injected_fault: copywriter's site_copy.about states 'grew the platform to over 2 million users' as fact, and lists it in claim_refs as 'achievement_signals[3]', but client_research.achievement_signals only has indices 0-2 and none of them support that number -- the reference does not resolve to any claim_status:'supported' finding

steps: researcher returns client_research with achievement_signals[0..2], all claim_status 'supported', usable_voice_excerpt_count = 4 (voice floor satisfied, isolating this case to the sourcing check specifically)
brand_strategist's positioning_brief.proof_points only cite achievement_signals[0..2]
copywriter's site_copy (fault injection) adds a fourth, unsourced claim to 'about' and cites a non-existent 'achievement_signals[3]' in claim_refs
builder renders site_draft with pages[].claim_refs mirroring site_copy.claim_refs, including the bad reference

voice_qa's claim_sourcing check attempts to resolve every claim_ref against client_research and positioning_brief.proof_points; 'achievement_signals[3]' does not exist and the claim has no other traceable source_ref -- fails
assertions: voice_qa_report.checks.claim_sourcing.unsourced_claims includes an entry for the '2 million users' claim with location referencing site_copy.about (or the equivalent rendered page)

voice_qa_report.checks.structural_voice_match.result and lexical_fingerprint_match.result may independently be 'pass' -- this case isolates claim_sourcing as the sole failing check, proving zero-tolerance sourcing is enforced even when voice/structure are otherwise fine
no deployed_site artifact exists for client_unsourced_002 at this point
a repair that drops the unsourced claim or rephrases it as generic, unattributed framing (never re-adding a fabricated source_ref to force a pass) is the only valid fix per ANTI-FABRICATION

name: Sparse GitHub signal: metrics_researcher returns a thin/empty pass and content_strategist falls back to trend_signal for sourcing

setup.injected_fault: client's GitHub org has had only two uncorroborated commits since since_ts and no releases or product_usage signal this cycle -- a genuinely quiet cycle, not a tool failure

steps: metrics_researcher polls github_commits, github_releases, and product_usage for client_sparse_github_003 (once the forthcoming github_activity_research tool exists; treated as unavailable/no-findings honestly in the interim per its Failure behavior section)

metrics_researcher emits metrics_signal(signal_source:'github_commits') with findings tagged claim_status:'needs_source' for the two uncorroborated commits (never 'supported', per its rule that raw commit volume alone never clears the bar), and is_event_trigger:false; emits no github_releases or product_usage artifact since neither has findings this cycle

metrics_researcher records record_fleet_event(event_type:'metrics_polled') summarizing the thin pass -- it does not retry or invent findings to fill the gap, and it has no verifier-repair loop of its own
trend_researcher independently returns trend_signal with industry_findings[0] (claim_status:'supported', a real sourced industry data point) for the same cadence tick

content_strategist reads both signals, finds metrics_signal has no claim_status:'supported' finding to cite, and falls back to trend_signal.industry_findings[0] as the post_brief's signal citation instead, picking a content_pillar this trend genuinely fits
assertions: metrics_signal's only finding(s) are claim_status:'needs_source', never 'supported' -- content_strategist correctly treats the metrics signal as non-citable rather than stretching a commit count into a hard claim

post_brief.pillar is a current member of ClientBrandMemory.content_pillars and is chosen because trend_signal.industry_findings[0] genuinely supports it, not because content_strategist forced the metrics-thin pillar to fit
content_strategist does not report this cycle as skipped-for-thin-signal, because trend_signal alone satisfies the sourcing requirement for a non-milestone post_type

no metrics_signal finding is fabricated or upgraded from needs_source to supported to give content_strategist something to cite -- the fallback to trend_researcher is the correct, policy-compliant path, not a workaround

name: Pillar invention rejected: content_strategist may only select existing ClientBrandMemory.content_pillars, never add one

setup.injected_fault: content_strategist drafts a post_brief with pillar = 'AI agent tooling takes' -- a topic that is trending in trend_signal but is NOT a member of ClientBrandMemory.content_pillars at version 2

steps: content_strategist reads ClientBrandMemory version 2 (content_pillars = ['infra reliability lessons', 'migration war stories']) and this cycle's trend_signal, which has a strong, well-sourced industry_findings entry about 'AI agent tooling'

content_strategist (fault injection) attempts to write post_brief.pillar = 'AI agent tooling takes', a value that does not exist in ClientBrandMemory.content_pillars, treating a hot trend as license to add a pillar on the fly

the manager/harness validates post_brief.pillar against ClientBrandMemory.content_pillars at based_on_memory_version before handing the brief to ghostwriter, and rejects the brief because the pillar is not a current member

content_strategist is required to either map the trend to an existing pillar (e.g. framing the AI-agent-tooling angle through 'infra reliability lessons' if it genuinely fits) or, if no existing pillar fits, skip the cycle as skipped-for-thin-signal rather than inventing a new one
assertions: no post_brief with pillar = 'AI agent tooling takes' (or any value absent from ClientBrandMemory.content_pillars at based_on_memory_version) is ever handed to ghostwriter

ClientBrandMemory.content_pillars and .version for client_pillar_invent_004 are unchanged by this cycle -- content_strategist has no write access to ClientBrandMemory under any circumstance, including a 'good idea' trend-driven pillar
if content_strategist cannot honestly map the trend onto an existing pillar, the correct outcome is a skipped-for-thin-signal record_fleet_event, never a fabricated or auto-added pillar

adding a new content pillar for this client, if ever warranted, requires a brand_strategist pass (site-build or explicit re-brand) that writes a new ClientBrandMemory version -- content_strategist cannot shortcut that boundary

name: Viral-pattern guardrail (positive case): a thread-starter drafted against a viral_pattern shape still passes claim_sourcing because every stated claim resolves to a supported source
steps: trend_researcher returns trend_signal.viral_pattern_findings[0] (pattern_id 'vp_1', hook_style 'numbered-countdown', format 'thread', structural_notes describing the shape only, observed_post_url present, and -- correctly -- no claim_status field at all) alongside a real, sourced trend_signal.industry_findings[0]

content_strategist produces post_brief with post_type 'thread-starter', format 'thread', based_on_pattern_ref = 'trend_signal.viral_pattern_findings[0]' for shape only, AND based_on_trend_signal_ref = 'trend_signal.industry_findings[0]' as the required real signal citation (per its sourcing requirement, based_on_pattern_ref never substitutes for a real signal citation)

ghostwriter drafts post_draft.text borrowing vp_1's numbered-countdown structural shape, but every specific number/claim in the text is drawn from client_research achievement_signals and trend_signal.industry_findings[0] -- never from vp_1's observed_post_url content -- and claim_refs lists only those resolvable references

voice_qa runs structural_voice_match, lexical_fingerprint_match, and claim_sourcing for content_type 'post'; claim_sourcing resolves every entry in claim_refs to a claim_status:'supported' finding with no unresolved claim; all three checks pass
assertions: post_draft.claim_refs contains zero references to trend_signal.viral_pattern_findings -- the pattern entry supplied structure only and is never cited as a claim source, per artifacts.md section 9's guardrail

voice_qa_report.checks.claim_sourcing.result = 'pass' even though the post_type and structural shape were directly informed by a viral_pattern_findings entry -- borrowing a proven hook/format shape does not, by itself, trigger a claim_sourcing failure

no number, quote, or statistic appearing in post_draft.text traces back to vp_1.observed_post_url -- every factual assertion traces to client_research or trend_signal.industry_findings with claim_status:'supported'

this case is the guardrail's positive control: viral_pattern_claim_leak style cases prove the check catches a lifted claim; this case proves the same check does not falsely penalize a legitimately shape-only borrowing

name: Dual-platform publish: X succeeds and LinkedIn fails, surfaced as independent per-platform status, never a single boolean

setup.precondition: voice_qa_report.gate_result = 'pass' for content_type 'post'; a single-post WhatsApp approval has been granted (approval_mode 'single')
setup.injected_fault: publish_x succeeds and returns a post_url; publish_linkedin fails with a rate_limited error from the LinkedIn API

steps: publisher calls publish_x for client_dual_platform_006's approved post_draft, receives status 'published' with post_url and published_at populated, error null
publisher calls publish_linkedin independently (Slice 6a assumed live for this client), receives status 'failed' with error = 'rate_limited', post_url and published_at both null

publisher writes published_post.platform_status with BOTH platform entries populated to their true, independently-observed states -- it does not wait for LinkedIn to retry before recording X's success, and does not mark X as anything but 'published' because LinkedIn failed
publisher records a fleet event carrying the full platform_status object (both keys) rather than a single summary boolean, since at least one platform succeeded but not both
assertions: no field anywhere on published_post collapses platform_status.x and platform_status.linkedin into a single top-level 'published' boolean, per artifacts.md section 12 and policy.md's PUBLISH STATUS rule

the manager's fleet status roll-up and any WhatsApp publish-confirmation message name both platforms' states explicitly for client_dual_platform_006, never reporting a bare 'published' on the strength of X alone

publisher does not silently auto-retry publish_linkedin inline -- a LinkedIn retry requires either the bounded content-loop repair path or a fresh human decision, not an internal retry hidden inside this publish call

published_post.approved_by_approval_id is the single-use approval for this exact draft and is not reused if a later manual LinkedIn-only retry is issued -- a fresh approval would be required for that retry per policy.md
